Yet, some posts beat the odds, getting tons of likes and becoming famous on Instagram. These iconic posts, often from celebrities, prove that creativity and fandom know no limits. If we look at the Indian Instagram scene, cricketer Virat Kohli's latest post celebrating the men's team's ICC T20 World Cup victory has become the most-liked Instagram post from India. At the time of publishing the post has garnered more than 18.5 million likes on the platform. Skins is one of the projects, that XXXTentacion worked on before his death, and features appearances from Travis Barker of Blink-182 and Kanye West. 9. Instagram has over 500 million active daily users. Every second of every day, people like posts from their favorite brands, friends, celebrities, and influencers. While some users battle for the most likes through posting content, others simply enjoy engaging with the content on their feed. But why do likes really matter? Well, Instagram helps drive the economy. The highly followed influencers you know and love use their platforms to share products and brands straight to your feed. Likes fuel the economy through the power of marketing. If you were wondering what posts receive the most attention on Instagram, you have come to the right place. In this blog, we will give you a rundown on the most liked Instagram posts. Top 10 Most Liked Instagram Posts 1. Lionel Messi Winning the World Cup In 2022, the most liked Instagram post was given a new face thanks to professional Argentinian soccer player, Lionel Messi. Messi ended up scoring a goal that ensured Argentina won the FIFA World Cup. In the post that now has 75.2 million likes, he is lifting the trophy into the air, presenting a face full of happiness and excitement. 2. World Record Egg On January 4, 2019, the world came together to break the world record for the most liked picture on Instagram by liking a photograph of an egg. With 61.1 million likes, the egg successfully became the most liked photo on Instagram by a landslide. The egg beat out Kylie Jenner’s most liked picture on Instagram by 35 million likes in January of 2019. The caption of the most liked post read, “Let’s set a world record together and get the most liked post on Instagram. Beating the current world record held by Kylie Jenner (18 million)! We got this.” 3. Lionel Messi with World Cup Trophy You’ll find that Lionel Messi is actually on this list quite a few times and most of the Instagram posts that he’s managed to get into the most likes on have to do with him winning the FIFA World Cup for Argentina in 2022.

baseline.final_answer: Lionel Messi holds the record for the most-liked Instagram post of all time, with his post celebrating Argentina's 2022 FIFA World Cup victory garnering over 75 million likes.
